insights.headline: EU AI Act High-Risk Delayed to 2027 — Pivot the Compliance Pitch
insights.analysis: The Digital Omnibus (May 7, 2026) deferred Annex III high-risk obligations to Dec 2, 2027, killing the 'August 2026 enforcement' urgency in prior briefings. But AI-content transparency/watermarking still lands Dec 2, 2026. Retire the deadline-scramble messaging and reposition NotVerify around the live Dec-2026 transparency hook plus continuous audit-readiness — ship AI-generated-translation labeling and audit export into Q3 GTM.

insights.headline: Rivals Are Commoditizing the 'Translate-Then-Verify' Wedge
insights.analysis: Smartling launched an automated LQA Agent, Lilt added first-party AI QA plus the autonomous 'LILT Assist' operator, and Lingo.dev v1 shipped an independent-reviewer QA architecture — all productizing NotVerify's core thesis, but grading their own output. Defend hard on what they can't credibly offer: independent, vendor-neutral, auditable Glass-Box trust scoring that sits over any engine. Lead sales with 'don't let the translator grade its own homework.'

insights.headline: MCP/A2A Now a Procurement Gate — and a Tuck-In Window Is Open
insights.analysis: Lilt shipped an industry-first MCP server + A2A integration, and the protocols are consolidating under the Linux Foundation (June spec adds recursive sub-agents) — MCP/A2A-native is becoming table stakes to stay in enterprise agent stacks. Ship NotVerify as an invokable QA sub-agent now. In parallel, regulation-aligned explainability tuck-ins (Iridius compliance-by-design, Bering Lab, Japan's ROZETTA) are reachable accelerants for the Glass Box roadmap.
